---
title: "prep_midterm"
format: html
editor: visual
bibliography: references.bib
---

# tutorial3
test using git command in terminal

# Tutorial 4: Figures, Tables, and Cross-Referencing
## Task 1: Working with Figures
```{r}
#| label: fig-penguins-scatter
#| fig-cap: "Body mass vs Flipper length for three penguin species"
#| warning: false

library(ggplot2)
library(palmerpenguins)

ggplot(penguins, aes(x = flipper_length_mm, y = body_mass_g, color = species)) +
  geom_point() +
  theme_minimal()
```
@fig-penguins-scatter shows the relationship between Body mass vs Flipper length for three penguin species.

## Task 2: Creating Professional Tables
```{r}
#| label: tbl-penguin-summary
#| tbl-cap: "Mean body mass of penguins by species and sex."

library(dplyr)

penguins |>
  filter(!is.na(sex)) |>
  group_by(species, sex) |>
  summarise(mean_mass = mean(body_mass_g)) |>
  knitr::kable()
```

@tbl-penguin-summary shows the statistics summary of Mean body mass of penguins by species and sex.

## Task 3: Section referencing {#sec-task3}

@sec-task3 introduce a section referencing.

## Task 4: Add image

![](https://www.worldatlas.com/r/w2560-q80/upload/a8/1b/ff/nz-01.png)

## Extension: Academic Referencing

*Parenthetical:* The data was originally collected by Gorman et al. [@penguins_data]

*Narrative:* @penguins_data argues that the increase of flipper length can increase the weight of penguins.
